Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing')."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"missing_data","description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":[],"properties":{"data_type":{"type":"string","description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters)."},"context":{"type":"string","description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"mention_owners","description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"}}]}}

Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

2026-04-02T05:51:16.6817066Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-02T05:51:00.82451317Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"57726","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-02T05:51:16.6829472Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-02T05:51:01.12739542Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57726,\"title\":\"[Azure Mgmt Generator] Safe flatten serialization issue\",\"body\":\"\\u0026gt; So this is what is happening:\\n\\u0026gt; the object has a \\u0026#34;safe-flattened\\u0026#34; property, because its enclosing type only have one property:\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;         internal WinRMConfiguration WinRM { get; set; }\\n\\u0026gt;         ///  The list of Windows Remote Management listeners. \\n\\u0026gt;         public IList WinRMListeners\\n\\u0026gt;         {\\n\\u0026gt;             get\\n\\u0026gt;             {\\n\\u0026gt;                 if (WinRM is null)\\n\\u0026gt;                     WinRM = new WinRMConfiguration();\\n\\u0026gt;                 return WinRM.Listeners;\\n\\u0026gt;             }\\n\\u0026gt;         }\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; Once one accesses property `WinRMListeners`, an instance of `WinRM` will be created.\\n\\u0026gt; There will be a difference in the request payload depending on the existence of `WinRM`.\\n\\u0026gt; If we never have called `WinRMListeners`, we get the following in the payload:\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; {\\n\\u0026gt; \\t// no \\u0026#34;winRM\\u0026#34; property at all.\\n\\u0026gt; }\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; if we have called `WinRMListeners` (even if we only read it to put the content into log, never add a new value into the list), since `WinRM` property will no longer be `null`, we get this in the request payload:\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; {\\n\\u0026gt; \\t\\u0026#34;winRM\\u0026#34;: {}\\n\\u0026gt; }\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; This behavior of our SDK that user\\u0026#39;s behavior would affect the state of an object is not good - logging frameworks might read properties and the user themselves may never know this, but it changes the request.\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; Another part of the causes of this issue is that the compute RP is throwing errors for payload like `\\u0026#34;winRM\\u0026#34;: {}`, this reads like they treat `listeners` as if it is required, but their spec does not document this. \\u0026lt;- this is an issue the service should take care of.\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; As a workaround, currently in the SDK code, there is no way that could allow you to \\u0026#34;not include `winRM`\\u0026#39;s empty object in the payload once you have read it, considering the service works as if `listeners` property is required, you could try to do this:\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; config.WinRMListeners.Clear();\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; to ensure this in the request:\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; {\\n\\u0026gt; \\t\\u0026#34;winRM\\u0026#34;: {\\n\\u0026gt; \\t\\t\\u0026#34;listeners\\u0026#34;: []\\n\\u0026gt; \\t}\\n\\u0026gt; }\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; maybe CRP could consider this as \\u0026#34;valid\\u0026#34;. \\n\\n _Originally posted by @ArcturusZhang in [#57563](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57563#issuecomment-4159654961)_\\n\\nAccessing the safe flattened property when it is a collection would change the state of the instance, and it will produce different payload.\\nWe need to fix this behavior.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57726\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"ArcturusZhang\",\"id\":10554446,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-02T05:48:57Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-02T05:49:53Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-02T05:51:16.6854311Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-02T05:51:01.327761542Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57726,\"title\":\"[Azure Mgmt Generator] Safe flatten serialization issue\",\"body\":\"\\u0026gt; So this is what is happening:\\n\\u0026gt; the object has a \\u0026#34;safe-flattened\\u0026#34; property, because its enclosing type only have one property:\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;         internal WinRMConfiguration WinRM { get; set; }\\n\\u0026gt;         ///  The list of Windows Remote Management listeners. \\n\\u0026gt;         public IList WinRMListeners\\n\\u0026gt;         {\\n\\u0026gt;             get\\n\\u0026gt;             {\\n\\u0026gt;                 if (WinRM is null)\\n\\u0026gt;                     WinRM = new WinRMConfiguration();\\n\\u0026gt;                 return WinRM.Listeners;\\n\\u0026gt;             }\\n\\u0026gt;         }\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; Once one accesses property `WinRMListeners`, an instance of `WinRM` will be created.\\n\\u0026gt; There will be a difference in the request payload depending on the existence of `WinRM`.\\n\\u0026gt; If we never have called `WinRMListeners`, we get the following in the payload:\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; {\\n\\u0026gt; \\t// no \\u0026#34;winRM\\u0026#34; property at all.\\n\\u0026gt; }\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; if we have called `WinRMListeners` (even if we only read it to put the content into log, never add a new value into the list), since `WinRM` property will no longer be `null`, we get this in the request payload:\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; {\\n\\u0026gt; \\t\\u0026#34;winRM\\u0026#34;: {}\\n\\u0026gt; }\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; This behavior of our SDK that user\\u0026#39;s behavior would affect the state of an object is not good - logging frameworks might read properties and the user themselves may never know this, but it changes the request.\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; Another part of the causes of this issue is that the compute RP is throwing errors for payload like `\\u0026#34;winRM\\u0026#34;: {}`, this reads like they treat `listeners` as if it is required, but their spec does not document this. \\u0026lt;- this is an issue the service should take care of.\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; As a workaround, currently in the SDK code, there is no way that could allow you to \\u0026#34;not include `winRM`\\u0026#39;s empty object in the payload once you have read it, considering the service works as if `listeners` property is required, you could try to do this:\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; config.WinRMListeners.Clear();\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; to ensure this in the request:\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt; {\\n\\u0026gt; \\t\\u0026#34;winRM\\u0026#34;: {\\n\\u0026gt;
